page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The memory subsystem consists of 192.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. Multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores all place in the top 10% of tested hardware. Memory performance is a notable strength, with read-only cached small block bandwidth reaching 11,096.49 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 201.25 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong tiers respectively. Additionally, while text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) LLM models is strong to top-tier, text generation for small (135M) models is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and eliminates hypervisor overhead, maximizing the utility of the 96 dedicated physical cores. The high multi-core performance and memory bandwidth make this server highly efficient for highly parallelized workloads. The c8g.metal-24xl is suited for multi-threaded compilation, high-throughput database operations, Redis in-memory caching, static web serving, and parallelized scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, it provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its high multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and multi-core Geekbench scores. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, reaching 11,096.49 GB/sec for cached small blocks. However, single-core performance is moderate, and text generation for small LLM models represents a performance bottleneck. Given its bare-metal architecture and high parallel processing capabilities, the c8g.metal-24xl is suited for software compilation, Redis caching, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores and 192 GB DDR5 RAM for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 server delivering exceptional multi-core throughput and DDR5 memory bandwidth for highly parallelized compute workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical ARM64 cores utilizing AWS Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.8 GHz.
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving.
